reforms.description: Finland aims to enhance cooperation between businesses and universities by using funding criteria for higher-education institutions or R&D vouchers, particularly to support start-ups. The government has created Team Finland to coordinate the activities of several institutions, focusing on internationalisation and maintaining strong government support for basic R&D and education. Additionally, Finland is exploring the conditions for expanding alternative financial instruments for SMEs, including crowdfunding and asset-based lending, to better support innovative firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing cooperation and funding for R&D is expected to stimulate innovation and support the growth of start-ups.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been put into practice as part of the government's ongoing support for R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms involve significant changes in funding and cooperation mechanisms but do not constitute a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: Use funding criteria for higher-education institutions or R&D vouchers, to reinforce co-operation between companies, particularly start-ups, and universities.

reforms.description: The government plans to support innovation and entrepreneurship by creating special funds to raise equity capital and enhance the risk-taking capacity of businesses, particularly start-ups and growth firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Creating special funds for equity capital is expected to enhance the capacity of start-ups to innovate and grow.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a major initiative to bolster the startup ecosystem through significant financial support.
reforms.source_quote: Beyond streamlining regulations, the government plans to support innovation and entrepreneurship in several ways. It is creating special funds to raise equity capital and enhance the risk-taking capacity of businesses, notably start-ups and growth firms.

reforms.description: The government intends to consolidate higher education institutions to create larger centres of excellence, which would enhance integration into international research networks and promote collaboration on innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Consolidating higher education institutions is expected to improve research capabilities and foster innovation through collaboration.
reforms.status_evidence: The intention to consolidate institutions has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the higher education landscape aimed at enhancing research and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Consolidation is necessary to create larger centres of excellence, with a higher profile which would favour integration in international research networks.

reforms.description: The government has announced a reduction in labour taxation, notably through an increase in the earned income deduction focusing on low and medium incomes, and coordination of tax and social security measures to reduce inactivity traps.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Reducing labour taxation is expected to incentivize work and innovation by making it more financially viable for individuals to engage in productive activities.
reforms.status_evidence: The reduction in labour taxation has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, this reform is more of an adjustment rather than a major overhaul of the tax system.
reforms.source_quote: The government has announced a reduction in labour taxation, notably through an increase in the earned income deduction focusing on low and medium incomes.

reforms.description: The Finnish government has announced a one-off package of €1.6 billion to fund key projects from 2016 to 2018, focusing on clean technologies, digitalisation, and health to encourage innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Funding key projects in clean technologies, digitalisation, and health is expected to stimulate innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding package has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This funding package represents a significant investment in priority areas for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: A one-off package of €1.6 billion to fund key projects in 2016-18 will also contribute to encourage innovation, in particular through investments in clean technologies, digitalisation and health.

reforms.description: The Finnish government has set an objective to allocate 5% of public procurement towards innovative products, aiming to stimulate demand for innovation and support firms in developing new solutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Allocating a portion of public procurement to innovative products is expected to stimulate demand and encourage firms to innovate.
reforms.status_evidence: The objective has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This objective is significant but does not represent a major overhaul of public procurement practices.
reforms.source_quote: The government has set an objective of 5% of innovative public procurement.

reforms.description: The Finnish government is exploring new forms of financing for innovative and fast-growing companies, including alternative financial instruments like crowdfunding and asset-based lending, to complement traditional funding sources.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Exploring new financing options is expected to enhance the ability of innovative firms to access capital and grow.
reforms.status_evidence: The exploration of new financing forms has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This exploration is significant but does not represent a major reform in financing policy.
reforms.source_quote: New forms of SME and entrepreneurship finance are emerging globally, including asset-based lending, alternative forms of debt, crowdfunding and hybrid instruments.
